---
title : "Polynomial regression in one dimension with a varying number of observations"
author : "Thimoté Dupuch"
date : today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme: zephyr
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo : false
#| output : false
using Pkg
Pkg.activate("..")
```


```{julia}
using CairoMakie
using Random
using Statistics
```


```{julia}
#| output : false
#| echo : false
CairoMakie.set_theme!(theme_latexfonts())
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "svg")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
colors = Makie.wong_colors()
blue, orange, red = colors[1], colors[2], colors[6]
```


```{julia}
#| output : false
true_fn(x) =  x .^ 2 .- 0.5
```


```{julia}
#| output : false
function sample_data(n, std_noise, rng)
    X = rand(rng, n) .* 2 .- 1
    Y = true_fn(X) .+ randn(rng, n) .* std_noise
    return X, Y
end
```
```{julia}
#| output : false
function fit_polynomial(X, Y, degree)
    Φ = reduce(hcat, [X .^ k for k in 0:degree])
    return (Φ' * Φ) \ (Φ' * Y)
end
```
```{julia}
#| output : false
function predict(X, w)
    Φ = reduce(hcat, [X .^ k for k in 0:(length(w)-1)])
    return Φ * w
end
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
function plot_polynomial_regression(; seed=1, std_noise=0.25, kmax=5)
    rng = MersenneTwister(seed)

    ns    = round.(Int, 10 .^ (1:0.25:3))
    Xgrid = collect(-1:0.001:1)
    Ygrid = true_fn(Xgrid)

    fig = Figure(size=(1000, 1000))

    for (idx, n) in enumerate(ns)
        row, col = fldmod1(idx, 3)
        ax = Axis(fig[row, col], limits=((-1, 1), (-1, 1.1)), xlabel=L"x", ylabel=L"y")

        Xsample, Ysample = sample_data(n, std_noise, rng)

        for degree in 0:kmax
            w    = fit_polynomial(Xsample, Ysample, degree)
            Ypred = predict(Xgrid, w)
            if degree == kmax
                lines!(ax, Xgrid, Ypred, color=red, linewidth=3)
                train_err = mean((predict(Xsample, w) .- Ysample) .^ 2)
                test_err  = std_noise^2 + mean((Ypred .- Ygrid) .^ 2)
                ax.title = "n=$n  train=$(round(train_err, digits=2))  test=$(round(test_err, digits=2))"
            end
        end

        lines!(ax, Xgrid, Ygrid, color=blue, linewidth=3)
        scatter!(ax, Xsample, Ysample, color=:black, marker=:xcross, markersize=6)
    end

    return fig
end
```
```{julia}
fig = plot_polynomial_regression()
```

## Convergence rate for polynomial regression


```{julia}
#| output : false
"""
Run `nrep` repetitions across all `ns` and `degrees`, returning
  - `insample_err[rep, n_idx]`  — fixed-design excess risk (vs noiseless truth)
  - `test_err[rep, n_idx]`      — random-design test MSE (includes σ²)
both evaluated at the *final* degree (kmax).
"""
function collect_errors(ns, kmax, std_noise, nrep, rng, Xgrid, Ygrid)
    insample_err = zeros(nrep, length(ns))
    test_err     = zeros(nrep, length(ns))
 
    for irep in 1:nrep, (in_, n) in enumerate(ns)
        Xsample, Ysample = sample_data(n, std_noise, rng)
        w     = fit_polynomial(Xsample, Ysample, kmax)
        Ypred = predict(Xgrid, w)
 
        insample_err[irep, in_] = mean((predict(Xsample, w) .- true_fn(Xsample)) .^ 2)
        test_err[irep, in_]     = std_noise^2 + mean((Ypred .- Ygrid) .^ 2)
    end
 
    return insample_err, test_err
end
```
 
```{julia}
#| output : false
function errorbar_loglog!(ax, log_ns, values; label)
    μ      = mean(values, dims=1)[:]
    σ      = std(values,  dims=1)[:]
    log_μ  = log10.(μ)
    lo     = log_μ .- log10.(max.(μ .- σ, 1e-12))
    hi     = log10.(μ .+ σ) .- log_μ
 
    errorbars!(ax, log_ns, log_μ, lo, hi, whiskerwidth=6)
    lines!(ax, log_ns, log_μ, label=label, linewidth=2)
end
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
function plot_excess_risk(; seed=1, std_noise=0.25, kmax=5, nrep=32)
    rng   = MersenneTwister(seed)
    ns    = round.(Int, 10 .^ (1:0.25:5))
    Xgrid = collect(-1:0.001:1)
    Ygrid = true_fn(Xgrid)
 
    insample_err, test_err = collect_errors(ns, kmax, std_noise, nrep, rng, Xgrid, Ygrid)
 
    log_ns      = log10.(ns)
    theory_line = log10.(std_noise^2 * (kmax + 1) ./ ns)
 
    fig = Figure(size=(700, 350))
 
    for (col, (values, excess_fn, title)) in enumerate([
        (insample_err, identity,              "Fixed design"),
        (test_err,     x -> x .- std_noise^2, "Random design"),
    ])
        ax = Axis(fig[1, col];
            title  = title,
            xlabel = L"\log_{10} (n)",
            ylabel = L"\log_{10} \text{(excess risk)}",
            limits = ((1, 5), (-6, 4)),
        )
 
        adjusted = excess_fn(values)
        errorbar_loglog!(ax, log_ns, adjusted; label="Excess risk")
        lines!(ax, log_ns, theory_line, color=red, linewidth=2, label=L"\sigma^2 d/n")
        axislegend(ax)
    end
 
    return fig
end
```


  
```{julia}
fig = plot_excess_risk()
```
